Parent.title: Parent
Parent.description: Represents a parent user on the platform.

children_ids.description: Array of child IDs associated with this parent.

status.description: The current status of the parent.

tags.description: Manual and automatic tags for filtering and segmentation.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the parent was created.

last_sale_date.description: Timestamp of the most recent sale.

total_sales_count.description: Total number of sales made by this parent.

notes.description: Manual notes added by an admin.

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student on the platform.

parent_id.description: The ID of the parent user.

course_type.description: The type of course the student is enrolled in.

teacher_id.description: The ID of the assigned teacher.

parent_id.description: The ID of the parent who made the purchase.

student_id.description: The ID of the student for whom the package was purchased.

package_type.description: The number of lessons in the package.

currency.description: Currency of the sale (e.g., EUR, USD).

trial_date.description: The scheduled date and time of the trial.

trial_status.description: The status of the trial lesson.

params.name: parentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent user.

definition.description: Stores student profiles. Grouped under a top-level collection for easier querying by admins.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sale.

params.name: trialId
params.description: The unique identifier for the trial lesson.

firestore.reasoning: The data structure is designed for a CRM-style admin panel. All major entities (parents, students, sales, trials) are top-level collections. This avoids deep nesting, simplifies security rules, and makes querying/filtering for the admin panel much more efficient. For example, admins can easily query all students or all sales without needing to know parent IDs. The relationships are maintained via ID fields (e.g., `parent_id` in the Student document).
